title: Bridge Essay: Conflicted between Goal-Directed and Habitual Control, an fMRI Investigation.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mechanicality — habit as automated behavior, loss of conscious control, automaticity.

**Conflicted between Goal-Directed and Habitual Control, an fMRI Investigation.**. eNeuro.. PMID: 30310863. DOI: 10.1523/ENEURO.0240-18.2018. "Slips of action" occur in everyday life when we momentarily lose sight of agoal (for example, when in arush or distracted). Associative models propose that these habitual responses can be activated via adirect stimulus-response (S-R) mechanism, regardless of the current hedonic value of the outcome.
